findings.evidence: `baseChangePointsIso_hom_apply_tmul` is already general at lines 153-157, while the two naturality simp lemmas specialize to `(1 ⊗ₜ[k] h)` at lines 209-214 and 223-229.
findings.fix: Generalize these pointwise forward naturality lemmas to include `(s : K)`, e.g. values at `(s ⊗ₜ[k] h)` with the corresponding `φ (s • f.ofConv h)` / `s • φ (f.ofConv h)` form, and derive the `1` cases only if needed.
findings.issue: `baseChangePointsIso_hom_mapPoints_apply` and `mapPoints_baseChangePointsIso_hom_apply` are stated only at `(1 ⊗ₜ[k] h)`, even though the forward base-change API just above is naturally on arbitrary `(s ⊗ₜ[k] h)`.

summary: The bundled iso is at the expected level, but the forward pointwise naturality API is unnecessarily specialized to the inclusion `h ↦ 1 ⊗ h` instead of arbitrary pure tensors.
